facts.text: Westbury was named in 1675 by English Quaker Henry Willis after his hometown of Westbury in Wiltshire, England.
The Long Island Railroad reached Westbury in 1840, turning the village into a hub for German, Italian, Irish, and Polish farmers.
In 1927, Charles Lindbergh launched his historic flight to Paris from Roosevelt Field, just a few hundred yards south of downtown Westbury.
Westbury’s historic Space at Westbury Theater, a restored 1920s venue, now hosts concerts, comedy shows, and community events.
The village’s first public library, now the Westbury Memorial Public Library, also houses the Historical Society of the Westburys.
Westbury’s early community was shaped by the Quaker faith, with Brigid’s Parish founded in the 17th century and the village’s history closely tied to the Friends.
